questions.question: Explain the difference between pass-by-value, pass-by-reference, and pass-by-pointer in C++ with an example each.
keyPoints: Pass-by-value: creates copy of object using copy constructor; changes don't affect original
Pass-by-reference: passes object alias using &; changes affect original; no copy overhead
Pass-by-pointer: passes address using *; allows NULL; explicit dereferencing needed; changes affect original
Example code showing each method with function signature

questions.question: What is object slicing in C++? How can it be prevented?
keyPoints: Object slicing: derived class object loses derived part when passed by value to base class parameter
Slicing removes virtual function table and derived members
Prevention methods: pass by reference, pass by pointer, use virtual functions with pointers/references
Example showing slicing and prevention

questions.question: Explain the role of copy constructor in passing objects to functions. When is it invoked?
keyPoints: Copy constructor initializes new object as copy of existing object
Invoked during: pass-by-value, returning by value, initialization of objects with =
Default copy constructor does shallow copy
User-defined copy constructor for deep copy when pointers exist
Destructor of copied object called when function ends (in pass-by-value)

questions.question: What are the problems with returning a reference from a function? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Problem: dangling reference when returning reference to local variable
Local variables destroyed when function returns
Accessing such reference causes undefined behavior
Example showing dangerous code and safe alternatives
Solutions: return by value, return reference to static, return reference to parameter

questions.question: Write a C++ program to demonstrate pass-by-reference to modify object data members.
keyPoints: Class definition with data members
Function with reference parameter (&)
Showing modification affects original object
Display before and after modification
Explanation of reference mechanism

questions.question: Explain shallow copy vs deep copy with respect to objects containing pointer members.
keyPoints: Shallow copy: copies pointer address, not pointed content; multiple objects share same memory
Deep copy: allocates new memory and copies pointed content; objects independent
Default copy constructor does shallow copy
Need user-defined copy constructor for deep copy
Memory leak and double deletion issues with shallow copy

questions.question: What is the significance of 'const' in function parameters? Explain const reference with example.
keyPoints: const prevents accidental modification of object
const reference: efficiency + safety (no copy, no modification
Allows passing both const and non-const objects
Compiler can optimize with const
Example showing const correctness and when to use

questions.question: Discuss all methods of passing objects to functions in C++ with their advantages and disadvantages. Include code examples for each method.
keyPoints: Pass by value: copy made; safe but slow for large objects; copy constructor/destructor called
Pass by reference: efficient; original modified; careful with const
Pass by pointer: allows NULL; explicit dereferencing; C compatibility
Comparison table of all methods
Code examples with class containing dynamic memory
Best practices recommendation

questions.question: Write a complete C++ program with a class having dynamically allocated memory. Implement proper copy constructor and demonstrate the problems of default shallow copy and solution using deep copy when passing objects to functions.
keyPoints: Class with pointer member and dynamic memory allocation
Default copy constructor causing shallow copy problems
User-defined copy constructor for deep copy
Functions demonstrating pass-by-value with both constructors
Output showing different behaviors
Explanation of copy constructor invocation

questions.question: Explain the concept of 'this' pointer in C++. How is it used in member functions? Write a program to demonstrate returning *this from a function for operator overloading.
keyPoints: this is implicit pointer to current object
Available in all non-static member functions
Used to access members and for returning reference to current object
Used in operator overloading for chaining
Program demonstrating this usage
Explanation of how it enables object modification through functions

questions.question: What is object slicing? Write a program with base and derived classes to demonstrate object slicing and show how to prevent it using pointers and references.
keyPoints: Object slicing definition with base and derived class
Demonstration of slicing when passing derived object by value
Virtual function table loss explanation
Prevention using: pass by reference, pass by pointer
Complete program with all cases
Output showing different behaviors
